response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASUREMENT OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A PHYSICAL PROPERTY THAT IS INDEPENDENT OF THE SIZE OR MASS OF THE SUBSTANCE. THE LEVEL OF TEMPERATURE AFFECTS THE RATE OF REACTION IN CHEMICAL PROCESSES. WHEN TWO SUBSTANCES ARE MIXED, THE TEMPERATURE OF THE MIXTURE CAN CHANGE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN BE MEASURED USING A THERMOMETER. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IS REGULATED BY THE BODY'S INTERNAL TEMPERATURE REGULATION SYSTEM. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION CAN BE INCREASED BY ADDING HEAT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN BE DECREASED BY COOLING IT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN BE CHANGED BY ADDING OR REMOVING HEAT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM IS A KEY FACTOR IN MANY PHYSICAL AND CHEMICAL PROCESSES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN BE MEASURED USING A THERMOMETER.
